extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won the stage 20 time trial by attacking it after already securing teammate VINGEGAARD Jonas's second place through strategic pacing at checkpoints.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT Wout faster at second checkpoint than ASGREEN Kasper, indicating Jumbo-Visma prioritized TT after securing VINGEGAARD's GC position.
POGAČAR Tadej visibly conserved effort in final TT with 5:20 buffer, signaling Olympics preparation trumped marginal time gains.
Jumbo-Visma extracted three stage wins with only four remaining riders after ROGLIČ Primož abandon, revealing depth in remaining squad.
Rolling TT course favored VAN AERT's weight advantage over punchy first TT, exposing course profile dictates specialist advantage more than pure power.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: won TT as stated objective after sacrificing earlier opportunities, shows Jumbo peak timing when GC secured
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: gained significant margin over CARAPAZ Richard for second despite team being reduced to four riders
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: deliberately soft-pedaled final TT with Olympics one week away, revealing future prioritization
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: led intermediate splits against specialists before VAN AERT, confirms top-tier TT capability outside Quick-Step leadout role
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: thirteenth in TT despite winning previous TDF TT in yellow, signals form decline from earlier tour dominance
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: lost significant time to VINGEGAARD for podium spot, indicating TT weakness exploitable in GT final weeks
